ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Mar-13-2012 Continued To Jul-10-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for Borel Private Bank. Stephen Ink from Borel Bank also present. Mary Jane Gordon for Thomas Jefferson. Sepideh Hirmand appeared by telephone for Julia Carter (Guardian for Minor).

Mr. Leutza explained how he calculated fees. Discussions were held on which value should be used for calculation of fees. The accounting petition was continued to July 10, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of an amended petition. Status of the family allowance was taken off calendar. Ms. Hirmand will appear by telephone.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Watts.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Further Status Of The Family Allowance Amount
Off Calendar Mar-13-2012 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Further Status Of The Family Allowance Amount
Off Calendar Jan-09-2012 Continued To Mar-13-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for Borel Private Bank. Mary Jane Gordon for Thomas Jefferson. Mina Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter. Stephen Ink from Borel Bank also present.

Ms. Sirkin stated that all Parties signed the stipulation regarding the family allowance and that it had been sent to the Court for filing last week. Mr. Leutza stated that the litigation matter with Gateway Mortgage had a hearing date of June 25, 2012 and that the accounting petition was filed today with a hearing date of March 13, 2012. Mr. Ink stated that there was no interest in the Jazz Club or the Lousiana property. Further status of the family allowance was continued to March 13, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Further Status Of The Family Allowance Amount
Off Calendar Dec-12-2011 Continued To Jan-09-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for Borel Private Bank. Mary Jane Gordon for Thomas Jefferson. Mina Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter. Stephen Ink from Borel Bank also present.

Mr. Leutza stated that he could have the accounting and the final inventory filed within three weeks. The Court will allow the family allowance to continue until the next hearing date. Counsel can enter into a stipulation for the family allowance to continue until close of the Estate if all Parties agree. The matter was continued to January 9, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the family allowance, and for filing of the accounting and the final inventory.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Brown.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Further Status Of The Family Allowance Amount
Off Calendar Jun-13-2011 Continued To Dec-12-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for Borel Bank. Mary Jane Gordon for Tom Jefferson and Limas Jefferson. Mina Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter. Stephen Ink Vice President of Borel Bank also present.

The Court noted that no one was present from Mr. Silver's office for Ollie B'Lanton. No one objected to the continuance of the family allowance. Mr. Ink stated that they were still working on the assets (Yoshie's Club, wine bottles, mineral interest in Louisiana) and that they do not have an ancilary proceeding plan at this point. Mr. Ink also stated that they were not going to pursue the unclaimed property due to costs, that the real property was foreclosed on and that Gateway Mortgage (CGC-11-507951) was not willing to settle. Furhtermore, Mr. Ink and Mr. Leutza stated that they anticipated that a final inventory would not be filed no sooner than six months as well as a six month period for closing of the Estate, spoke of the bank accounts, the Creditors, the Franchise Tax Board issue and the City and County of San Francisco claims. The matter was continued to December 12, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status/termination of the family allowance and for further discussions regarding the inventory and appraisal and close of the estate. Ms. Sirkin is to prepare the order extending the family allowance to December 12, 2011. The Court encouraged Counsel to comminicate with one another with regard to any questions/concerns they may have prior to the next hearing date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Further Status Of The Family Allowance Amount
Off Calendar Dec-13-2010 Continued To Jun-13-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alan Silver for Ollie B'Lanton. John Leutza for Borel Bank. Stephen Ink from Borel Bank. Minna Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter.

Mr. Leutza stated that the Bank is still in the process of collecting assets, that they were prepared to file a first partial inventory and that they would get it to the Referee within a week. Mr. Ink reported as to the status of the assets. Ms. Sirkin expressed concern about the assets being sold without being inventoried, how they were being marketed, what efforts had been made, etc. The Parties agreed to a $500.00 monthly family allowance. The Court ordered the family allowance for another six months at $500.00 a month and asked that the Auctioneers hold off on sale of the property until the items are appraised (stipulated by all Parties). Ms. Sirkin is to prepare the family allowance order and the stipulation regarding the appraisal. The matter was continued to June 13, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status. Ms. Sirkin will appear by telephone.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition For Family Allowance
Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for Borel Bank. Stephen Ink from Borel Bank. Alan Silver for Ollie B'Lanton. Mary Jane Gordon for Thomas Jefferson and Limas Jefferson. Mina Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter (Guardian for Henry Jefferson).

Ms. Sirkin stated that they were asking for $11,000.00 to pay for the Minor's preschool and that the other sums do not exceed $5,000.00. Mr. Leutza stated that the Estate does not have any assets to pay for any family allowance, that the Estate has $55,000.00 in liquid assets, that there may not be enough to cover the administration fees. In addition, Mr. Leutza stated that they had no idea what the Ferry Building investment was worth, that the jazz club had no value and that the Estate was in debt for $1,000,000.00. Mr. Silver and Mr. Ink also stated that the Estate did not have the assets to pay for a family allowance. Ms. Gordon had no comment. Ms. Sirkin stated that the IRS withdrew their request for payment, that they reduced the amount to $26,000.00, that no Inventory had been filed, that if the Court denies the petition, that she would request the Court wait to see if any other Creditor's Claims get filed and that she would request $2,000.00 to get the Minor through. The matter was taken under submission with the Court leaning toward granting the petition with a small amount retroactive to the date of death. Ms. Sirkin will submit an order leaving the dollar amount blank. Further status of the family allowance amount was set for December 13, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Sirkin will appear by telephone.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Petition For Family Allowance
Off Calendar Jul-12-2010 Continued To Aug-16-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for Thomas Jefferson and Limas Jefferson. Alan Silver for Ollie B'Lanton. John Leutza for Borel Bank. Stephen Ink from Borel Bank. Mina Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter. Barbara Jefimoff (Interested Party) aslo present.

The Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Mina Sirkin is denied. The Petition for Letters of Administration with will annexed filed by Mr. Silver is granted (subject to a written order). The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Petition for Family Allowance. Ms. Sirkin provided information regarding the expenses. The Court stated that Mr. Leutza needs to file his objection, that Ms. Srikin needs to file and serve documentation with regard to rent, utilities, payment for school for Henry only, expenses for the Nanny, all documents related to expenses and that it would consider the Petition for Family Allowance once said documents are filed. Ms. Sirkin's documents are to be filed by July 23, 2010. Oppositions are to be filed by August 6, 2010. Responses are to be filed by August 11, 2010. The Petition for Family Allowance was continued to August 16, 2010 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court did not allow Ms. Sirkin's request for temporary family allowance.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Y. Watts.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-25-2010 Continued To Jul-12-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for Borel Bank. Stephen Ink Trust Officer from Borel Bank. Mina Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter (Guardian).

Mr. Leutza stated that Mr. Silver did not feel it was necessary for him to appear today. Mr. Leutza also presented a stipulation signed by Mr. Silver, Ms. Sirkin and Himself with regard to an extension of time of the Special Administration and asked that the Trustee be granted general powers. Ms. Sirkin stated that she agreed to the request for general powers as long as the will was not admitted. Mr. Leutza will prepare an order indicating general powers and that special administration of the estate is to conitnue to until further order of the Court. The matter was continued to July 12, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard with the petition for Petition for Family Allowance. Ms. Sirkin will appear by telephone. Mr. Sirkin is to prepare a stipulation with regard to his request for limited authority under independent admnistration of the estate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Ex Parte Application For Letters Of Special Administration
Off Calendar Mar-26-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for Thomas Jefferson and Limas Jefferson. Alan Silver for Ollie B'lanton. Mina Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter.

Counsel presented opral argument to support their positions. The Court granted the petition appointing Borel Private Bank & Trust as the Special Administrator without full authority and no special powers except for the power to secure the real property and marshal the personal assets, and the power to negotiate with mortgage lenders regarding the Estate's real property, (signing the order for the same in open court). The Special Letters will expire on April 27, 2010. The property cannot be listed until the Court is able to review the listing agreement. Counsel for Borel Private Bank & Trust can file an ex parte petition regarding the right to sell the property which will require a copy of the listing aggreement. Ms. Carter's petition and Ms. B'Lanton's petition were continued to April 27, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Sirkin will appear by telephone.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Continued To Apr-05-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mina Sirkin appeared by telephone for Julia Carter. Mary Jane Gordon for and with Thomas Jefferson and with Limas Jefferson (Brothers). Alan Silver for and with Ollie B'Lanton.

Mr. Silver stated that he would file a petition for probate of will this afternoon or tomorrow. Ms. Gordon stated that she would object to the petition if it is presented on an intestacy basis. Ms. Sirkin stated that she may have an objection to Mr. Silver's petition and that she needed to examine the will. The matter was continued to April 5, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of Ms. B'Lanton's petition.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.
